growth_records.medium: JW1 (isolation medium), salinity varied by proportional dilution of major ions
growth_records.cite: Savoie et al. Ecophysiology of the Cosmopolitan OM252 Bacterioplankton (Gammaproteobacteria). mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00276-21. [PMC8269220]
growth_records.snippet: LSUCC0096 grew in salinities between 5.79 and 63.5, with a maximum growth rate of 0.23 (±0.01) doublings/h at 11.6 under the proportional scheme.
growth_records.notes: Paper reports maximum growth rate as 0.23 (±0.01) doublings/h at salinity 11.6 (proportional-ion-dilution scheme). Converted to natural-log specific growth rate: mu(h-1) = doublings/h x ln2 = 0.23 x 0.6931 = 0.159 h-1. Doubling time = 1/(doublings/h) = 1/0.23 = 4.35 h. Salinity growth experiments were stated to be conducted 'at room temperature' (exact °C not given); all other medium components (carbon, iron, phosphate, nitrogen, vitamins, trace metals) held constant.

growth_records.cite: Savoie et al. Ecophysiology of the Cosmopolitan OM252 Bacterioplankton (Gammaproteobacteria). mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00276-21. [PMC8269220]
growth_records.snippet: LSUCC0096 also grew between 12°C and 35°C in the isolation medium JW1 with a maximum growth rate of 0.36 (±0.06) doublings/h at 35°C
growth_records.notes: Paper reports maximum growth rate 0.36 (±0.06) doublings/h at 35°C in JW1 medium. Converted: mu(h-1) = 0.36 x ln2 = 0.36 x 0.6931 = 0.250 h-1. Doubling time = 1/0.36 = 2.78 h. Temperature range tested in triplicate, nonshaking, using JW1 isolation medium.

growth_records.cite: Savoie et al. Ecophysiology of the Cosmopolitan OM252 Bacterioplankton (Gammaproteobacteria). mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00276-21. [PMC8269220]
growth_records.notes: Paper reports 'typical' growth rate 0.19 (±0.03) doublings/h at 25°C in JW1 medium. Converted: mu(h-1) = 0.19 x ln2 = 0.19 x 0.6931 = 0.132 h-1. Doubling time = 1/0.19 = 5.26 h.

growth_records.medium: JW1 medium with all carbon sources and vitamins (chemoorganoheterotrophic positive control)
growth_records.cite: Savoie et al. Ecophysiology of the Cosmopolitan OM252 Bacterioplankton (Gammaproteobacteria). mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00276-21. [PMC8269220]
growth_records.snippet: The positive controls from the fourth transfer had an average growth rate of 0.20 ± 0.01 doublings/h, which is similar to growth rates found under normal growth conditions
growth_records.notes: Positive-control (heterotrophic) growth rate during 4th of 4 consecutive transfers = 0.20 ± 0.01 doublings/h in normal JW1 medium with all carbon sources/vitamins. Converted: mu(h-1) = 0.20 x ln2 = 0.139 h-1; doubling time = 1/0.20 = 5.0 h. Temperature not explicitly restated for this experiment (other physiological assays in the study were run at room temperature or as specified).

growth_records.medium: modified JW1 medium, chemolithoautotrophic (no organic carbon except trace vitamins, 100 uM thiosulfate as sole electron donor, bicarbonate as carbon source/buffer)
growth_records.cite: Savoie et al. Ecophysiology of the Cosmopolitan OM252 Bacterioplankton (Gammaproteobacteria). mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00276-21. [PMC8269220]
growth_records.snippet: whereas the experimental replicates had a much slower average growth rate of 0.07 ± 0.01 doublings/h (Fig. S9C).
growth_records.notes: Chemolithoautotrophic growth (thiosulfate as sole electron donor, bicarbonate as carbon source) during 4th of 4 consecutive transfers: 0.07 ± 0.01 doublings/h. Converted: mu(h-1) = 0.07 x ln2 = 0.0485 h-1; doubling time = 1/0.07 = 14.29 h. Demonstrates facultative chemolithoautotrophy; growth ~3x slower than heterotrophic positive control (0.20 doublings/h) despite only ~12% lower Gibbs free energy yield, attributed by authors to costlier biosynthesis from inorganic starting materials.
